result.stdout:
HttPlaceholder 2024.12.27.10 - (c) 2026 Ducode
Run this application with argument '-h' or '--help' to get more info about the command line arguments.
When running in to trouble, or just see what's going on, run this application with argument '-V' or '--verbose' to print the configuration variables.
You can also set the "verbose" environment variable (without quotes) to "true" to enable verbose logging.
Visit https://httplaceholder.org/

Authentication:
--apiusername: the username for securing the REST API (e.g. user)
--apipassword: the password for securing the REST API (e.g. pass)

Configuration:
--configjsonlocation: the location of the config.json file. This JSON file contains all possible configuration settings and a default value per setting. You can copy this file to any location on your PC. Don't put the config file in the installation folder, because these files will be overwritten when an update is installed. (e.g. F:\httplaceholder\config.json)

Development:
--dev: enables "dev" mode, which is used specifically for running HttPlaceholder on your dev machine. It disables some security related features which are nice for local development. Do NOT enable this on a test / production server as it might pose a security risk. (e.g. user)

GUI:
--enableuserinterface: whether the user interface should be enabled or not. The user interface is, if enabled, located at http://localhost:PORT/ph-ui. (e.g. true)

Storage:
--inputfile: for input file, you can both provide a path to a .yml file (to load only that file) or provide a path to a folder containing .yml files (which will all be loaded in that case). (e.g. C:\path\to\stubsfolder or C:\path\to\stubsfolder,C:\path\to\file.yml for multiple paths)
--disablefilewatcher: by default, the OS file watcher will be used to keep track of the .yml stub files. If a problem occurs with the file watcher, you can enable this option to read and track the .yml stub files in the 'classic' way (default false). (e.g. true)
--oldrequestsqueuelength: the maximum number of HTTP requests that the in memory stub source (used for the REST API) should store before truncating old records. Default: 40. (e.g. 100)
--storeresponses: whether the responses as returned by HttPlaceholder should be stored. Default: false (e.g. true)
--cleanoldrequestsinbackgroundjob: whether the cleaning of old requests should be done in a background job. If set to true, will delete old requests in a background job that runs once in 5 minutes. If set to false, will clean old requests every time a request is handled. Default: true. (e.g. true)
--enablerequestlogging: whether stub requests should be logged to the terminal window. Default: false (e.g. false)
--filestoragelocation: a location where the stubs and requests are stored. This is enabled by default and, if you omit this property, the stubs will be saved in your user profile folder. Specify 'useInMemoryStorage' to only save stubs in memory. (e.g. C:\httplaceholder_storage)
--useinmemorystorage: whether stubs should only be saved in memory. The stubs and requests will be deleted after restarting the application. Default: false (e.g. false)
--mysqlconnectionstring: a connection string that needs to be filled in if you want to use MySQL (e.g. Server=localhost;Database=httplaceholder;Uid=httplaceholder;Pwd=httplaceholder;Allow User Variables=true)
--sqliteconnectionstring: a connection string that needs to be filled in if you want to use SQLite (e.g. Data Source=C:\tmp\httplaceholder.db)
--sqlserverconnectionstring: connection string that needs to be filled in if you want to use MSSQL (e.g. Server=localhost,2433;Database=httplaceholder;User Id=sa;Password=Password123)
--postgresconnectionstring: a connection string that needs to be filled in if you want to use Postgres (e.g. Host=localhost,5432;Username=postgres;Password=postgres;Database=httplaceholder;SearchPath=public)

Stub:
--maximumextradurationmilliskey: the number of milliseconds the 'extraDuration' response writer can make a request wait at most. Defaults to 1 minute (60.000 millis). (e.g. 60000)
--healthcheckonrooturl: whether the root URL of HttPlaceholder (so "/") can be configured as stub or always returns 200 OK. Defaults to false. (e.g. true)
--allowglobalfilesearch: whether HttPlaceholder is able to accept any path for the 'file response writer'. If set to false, HttPlaceholder is only allowed to look for files relative to the .yml stub files or the file storage location. 'false' by default and not recommended to set to 'true' if you are running HttPlaceholder outside of a Docker container. (e.g. false)
--enablereverseproxy: whether to enable the reverse proxy response writer. (e.g. true)
--allowedhosts: configures the hostnames that are allowed in the reverse proxy response writer. Full strings and regular expressions are allowed. (e.g. ^google\.com$,www.google.com)
--disallowedhosts: configures the hostnames that are NOT allowed in the reverse proxy response writer. Full strings and regular expressions are allowed. (e.g. ^reddit\.com$,www.reddit.com)

Web:
--httpsport: the port HttPlaceholder should run under when HTTPS is enabled. Listen on multiple ports by separating ports with comma. (e.g. 5050)
--pfxpassword: the password for the .pfx file which should be used in the case HTTPS is enabled (e.g. 112233)
--readproxyheaders: whether the proxy headers 'X-Forwarded-For', 'X-Forwarded-Host' and 'X-Forwarded-Proto' should be taken into account when determining the IP, hostname and protocol (e.g. true)
--safeproxyips: the proxy IPs which are considered safe when reading the 'X-Forwarded-For', 'X-Forwarded-Host' and 'X-Forwarded-Proto' headers. Localhost is always permitted. Separate multiple IPs by using a comma. (e.g. 1.1.1.1,2.2.2.2)
--pfxpath: the path to the .pfx file in the case HTTPS is enabled. When no path is provided, the default .pfx file is used (e.g. C:\path\to\privatekey.pfx)
--port: the HTTP port HttPlaceholder should run under. Listen on multiple ports by separating ports with comma. (e.g. 5000)
--usehttps: whether HTTPS should be enabled or not. Default: false (e.g. true)
--publicurl: specifies the URL on which this HttPlaceholder instance can be reached. Useful if running behind a reverse proxy. (e.g. https://example.com/stubs)

Example: httplaceholder --apiusername user --apipassword pass
